current_implemented_build: V55.3 TrustMap Registry and Visual Object Stabilization
patch_type: TrustMap registry stabilization over V55.2, preserving V55.2 visual behavior while adding registry source-of-truth, visual style guide, visual references, and registry validation metadata
release_chain_status: V55.3 is the current implemented build. It loads after V55.2 in src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js. It preserves the radar-constellation TrustMap, clean CyberShield Core shield boundary, baseline Layer 1 operating domains, Purpose Protocol integration, and no-new-top-level-tabs rule. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
version_label_rule: Build and version labels belong in Settings/admin metadata and repo documentation only. They must not appear as executive-facing dashboard content.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V55.3. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
core_thesis: CyberShield evaluates whether the information behind a critical action or claim can be trusted before the organization acts, cites, shares, briefs, teaches, or relies on it.
core_question: Can we trust the information behind this action or claim before the organization acts?
trust_model_doctrine: Executives rarely have perfect information. CyberShield is not about perfect evidence. Evidence supports the Trust Model, but evidence volume is not the point. Information reliability, source confidence, owner accountability, verification path, consequence if wrong, and decision reliance are the point.
trustmap_doctrine: The CyberShield TrustMap is a radar-constellation interface, not a process-flow diagram. CyberShield Core is a clean neon shield boundary and trust kernel, not a normal node. Complexity lives at the edge. Trust is established at the center. Layer 1 domains are neon-blue graphical trust objects. Baseline Layer 1 domains must stay visible.
v55_3_changes: validates registry presence of CyberShield Core, baseline Layer 1 domains, and scenario lenses
keeps Internet Trust Engine as a documented future requirement track, not a live feature
documents GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy

trustmap_registry.current_boundary: Registry validates and documents TrustMap data. V55.2 remains the active visual behavior layer until a later build consumes the registry as the rendering source.

purpose_protocol_track.status: implemented as static advisory scaffold in V55 and preserved in V55.3
purpose_protocol_track.supporting_principle: Purpose is not governance until it can cause a refusal.
purpose_protocol_track.priority_scenario: Vendor payment destination change: if banking details changed within 30 days, payment approval is refused unless current banking verification and controller approval are present.
purpose_protocol_track.boundary: Static advisory prototype only. No live banking verification, live payment blocking, live controller workflow, backend email, CRM, ticketing, or enforcement.

avoid_framing: artifact-level trust score as the MVP anchor
internet_trust_engine_future_track.core_question: Do we have sufficient trust in this information for the decision, action, briefing, citation, or reliance purpose in front of us, and what is the risk if we are wrong?
internet_trust_engine_future_track.chain: Artifact -> Wrapper -> Transcript -> Sentence -> Claim -> Evidence -> Confidence -> Decision Impact -> Reliance Guidance -> Action
internet_trust_engine_future_track.sequence_note: Do not implement before TrustMap registry consumption and visual stabilization are complete.

recommended_next_build.label: V55.4 or V56 TrustMap Registry Consumption and CSS Reduction
goals: make TrustMap renderer consume data/trustmap/v55-3-trustmap-registry.json as the source of truth
move injected CSS toward stylesheet/module ownership
keep Internet Trust Engine as future track until TrustMap is stable

implemented_release_train.v52.2: Guided Onboarding, Routed Dashboard, and Layered TrustMap Patch

implemented_release_train.v52.7: TrustMap Navigation and Report Output System
implemented_release_train.v53: Trust Model and Deep Scenario Spine Build
implemented_release_train.v55.2: TrustMap Core Boundary and Constellation Polish
implemented_release_train.v55.3: TrustMap Registry and Visual Object Stabilization

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live evidence retrieval, internet claim verification, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: V55.3 validates the TrustMap registry but does not yet fully render TrustMap from the registry
V55.2 remains the active visual behavior layer
Layer 1 SVG/CSS icons are prototype approximations
reports download as text files rather than branded PDFs
contact capture is client-side only and not stored
scoring and trend lines are demo-directional and not statistically validated
Internet Trust Engine is documented as a future requirement track but is not yet implemented
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
